name: Ceftazidime
executiveSummary: Third-generation cephalosporin. Treatment of lower respiratory tract, skin and soft tissue, bone, joint, intra-abdominal, genitourinary, and central nervous system infections caused by susceptible gram-negative bacteria including Pseudomonas.
indications:
## Main indications

Third-generation cephalosporin. Treatment of lower respiratory tract, skin and soft tissue, bone, joint, intra-abdominal, genitourinary, and central nervous system infections caused by susceptible gram-negative bacteria including Pseudomonas.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.diluent: Reconst. 1 g with 10 mL SWFI, dilute in 50-100 mL and administer over 15-30 min.
adulto.administration: Direct IV: Yes. Reconst. 1 g in 10 mL SWFI and administer over 3-5 min. Intermittent IV: Yes.
adulto.notes: Irritant. Caution during reconstitution because CO₂ is released (remove bubbles before administration). Risk of phlebitis with direct IV route. IM administration is possible.

pediatrico.dose: Newborn: according to age and weight. Children: 100-150 mg/kg/day every 8 h, maximum dose: 6 g. Adults: 1-2 g every 8 h. Cystic fibrosis: 150-200 mg/kg/day every 6 h, maximum dose: 9 g. Uncomplicated urinary infection adults: 250 mg every 12 h; complicated: 500 mg every 12 h.
pediatrico.notes: In renal insufficiency adjust dose and monitor; risk of nephrotoxicity with concomitant nephrotoxic drugs. With prolonged treatment, risk of superinfection by non-susceptible organisms; risk of pseudomembranous colitis.

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 24 h refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- 6 h at room temperature and 24 h refrigerated.

## Pediatric guide

- Stable 24 h at room temperature protected from light; 10 days refrigerated.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Local intolerance, hypersensitivity, gastrointestinal disorders.
